summary: Building a WRAITH-themed classified intelligence app combining: (1) Mission Management Dashboard for tracking covert missions/threats/field reports, (2) Intelligence Hub with asset profiles/threat assessments/encrypted notes, and (4) Field Ops Briefing Tool for building and displaying mission briefings with objectives and HVT profiles.
architectural_notes: Themed as a classified/clandestine operations center with dark spy-thriller aesthetic
Operates under the fictional WRAITH universe (Title 50, plausible deniability lore)

existing_features.title: Create a Mission Management Dashboard where users can create, view, update, and delete covert missions. Each mission must include: codename, status (Active / Compromised / Completed / Aborted), threat level (Low / Elevated / Critical), assigned operatives, mission type (Recon / Cyber / Counter-Terror / In-Person Op), objectives list, and field reports (timestamped log entries). Display missions in a sortable, filterable list with status badges and threat indicators.

Build an Intelligence Hub module with three sub-sections: (a) Asset Profiles — create and manage profiles for field assets/operatives with codename, clearance level, specialization, status, and bio notes; (b) Threat Assessments — create threat entries with subject name, threat category, risk score (1–10), summary, and linked missions; (c) Encrypted Notes — create freeform classified notes with a title, body, classification tag (CONFIDENTIAL / SECRET / TOP SECRET / TS-SCI), and timestamp. All records must be stored and retrievable from the backend.

Implement a Field Ops Briefing Tool that allows users to compose and display structured mission briefings. Each briefing must include: operation codename, mission date, lead officer, objectives list (add/remove items), High-Value Target (HVT) profiles (name, description, threat tier), exfil routes (freeform text), rules of engagement, and classification level. Provide a 'Briefing View' mode that renders the briefing as a formatted classified document, suitable for presentation.

Apply a cohesive dark classified-ops visual theme across the entire application. Use a near-black background palette with high-contrast text (off-white/amber/red), monospaced or military-style typography, glowing accent elements suggesting digital/terminal aesthetics, classification banner strips at the top of sensitive views, and redaction-style UI components (e.g., blacked-out bars for high-sensitivity fields). Navigation should use a side panel or top bar styled as a classified operations interface. Apply the theme consistently to all three modules.

Create a new frontend Admin page (route: /admin) that is only accessible to authenticated users whose principal is recognized as admin by the backend isAdmin query. If a logged-in user is not an admin, display a styled 'ACCESS DENIED — UNAUTHORIZED CLEARANCE LEVEL' message matching the app's dark classified-ops theme. If the user is not logged in, redirect to the login screen.

Build the Admin page content panel (visible only to admins) with the following capabilities: (1) View the current list of admin principals, (2) Add a new admin by entering a principal ID string, (3) Remove an existing admin from the list (with confirmation dialog). Style the page consistently with the WRAITH dark classified-ops theme, using classification banner, monospaced typography, and amber/red accents.

new_feature_requests.summary: Mission Management Dashboard - track covert missions, threats, and field reports
Intelligence Hub - asset profiles, threat assessments, and encrypted notes
Field Ops Briefing Tool - build and display mission briefings with objectives and HVT profiles
Admin page with restricted access — only specific authorized users can access it

Add a backend admin authorization system. Maintain a hardcoded or storable list of admin principal IDs. Expose a query function `isAdmin(caller) : Bool` that returns true if the caller's principal is in the admin list. Expose a function `getAdminList() : [Principal]` and `addAdmin(p: Principal)` / `removeAdmin(p: Principal)` callable only by existing admins. Initialize the list with the canister deployer's principal.
Hardcode principal tppp7-aedx6-t5lmu-6qnik-e46l6-jbsl2-jmt6r-dhupo-bkdih-s2avm-zqe as an initial admin in the backend admin list

Hardcode principal `tppp7-aedx6-t5lmu-6qnik-e46l6-jbsl2-jmt6r-dhupo-bkdih-s2avm-zqe` as an initial admin in the backend admin list, alongside any existing initial admins (e.g., the canister deployer), so that this principal has admin access immediately upon deployment without needing to be added manually.
